license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgu9jwz6
title: Monoamine oxidase activity in fish brain tissue
author: Caio Maximino

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare tissue lysis buffer : 16.8 mM Na 2 HPO 4 and 10.6 mM KH 2 PO 4 , pH 7.4 , isotonized with sucrose Disodium phosphate Contributed by users Monopotassium phosphate Contributed by users sucrose Contributed by users

Prepare assay buffer : 168 mM Na 2 HPO 4 and 10.6 mM KH 2 PO 4 , pH 7.4 , isotonized with KCl Disodium phosphate Contributed by users Monopotassium phosphate Contributed by users Potassium Chloride Contributed by users
Euthanize fish in ice-cold water (<12 ºC), dissect brains in tissue lysis buffer. Two brains should be pooled per to form one sample unit.
Centrifuge samples on 0.5 mL tissue lysis buffer, at 1.000 x g for 5 min. (Centrifuge, -, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/3di2pne.png )
Reserve supernatants, which will be used for assays, and maintain On ice throughout the experiment.
Mix protein samples (approximately 100 µg) with 460 µL of assay buffer and preincubated at 37 °C for 00:05:00 (5 min).
Start reaction by adding 110 micromolar (µM) kynuramine hydrobromide in a final volume of 700 µL. Kynuramine hydrobromide Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #K3250
Incubate the final solution for 00:30:00 (30 min).

Stop reaction with 300 µL 10 % volume trichloroacetic acid. Tricholoracetic acid is a strong acid that causes severe skin burns and eye damage, and may cause respiratory irritation. All solutions using TCA should be made using safety googles and nitrile rubber gloves, and vapors avoided by making preparations under a fume hood.

Centrifuge reaction products at 16.000 x g for 00:05:00 (5 min) and 800 µL of the supernatants mixed with 1 mL NaOH ( 1 Molarity (M) . NaOH Contributed by users (Centrifuge, -, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/3di2pne.png )
Transfer centrifuged, NaOH-buffered reaction products to quartz cuvettes Standard Quartz cuvette Contributed by users
Transfer cuvettes to a spectrofluorophotometer Spectrofluorophotometer Contributed by users
Measure fluorescence using excitation at 315 nm and emission at 380 nm.
Concentration should be estimated with a standard curve of 4-hydroxyquinoline (4-Quinolinol) 4-Hydroxyquinoline (4-Quinolinol) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #H58005
Calculate enzyme activity as nmol 4-OH quinoline/min/mg protein (determined by the Bradford or, preferably, Lowry assays). Lowry Protein Assay by Irina Agarkova
